items.title: How to Remove a Malicious App on Your Android Phone
items.author: Dmitry Romanoff
items.content:
We've all been there. You accidentally click on an ad, and before you know it, a malicious app is installed on your phone. These apps often bombard you with constant, irrelevant, and obnoxious advertisements that are hard to close and distract from your daily phone use. What's worse is that these apps may masquerade as familiar, legitimate apps, making it difficult to detect and remove them.

In this article, I’ll share the method I used to remove one such app from a friend's Android phone. It involves finding the recently installed apps that could be causing the strange behavior and removing them. Let’s dive into the steps.

Step 1: Identify the App that Was Installed Recently

When you start noticing issues with your phone—like constant pop-up ads or random notifications—the first thing you should do is look at the apps installed around the time the issue started. It's often the most recently added app that is the culprit.

  1. Open the on your phone.
  2. Tap on your in the top-right corner.
  3. Select from the menu.

Step 2: Find Recently Installed Apps

Now, you’ll be able to see a list of apps installed on your phone. These apps are sorted by their most recent updates or installation dates. Look for any apps that were installed right around the time your phone started acting up. This is where the malicious app is likely hiding.

Step 3: Remove the Suspicious App

Once you’ve identified the app you suspect to be harmful, it’s time to remove it. Here’s how to uninstall it:

  1. Tap the app you suspect to be malicious.
  2. Select to remove the app from your device.

Step 4: Confirm the Issue Is Resolved

After uninstalling the suspicious app, restart your phone and check if the intrusive ads and abnormal behavior have stopped. If everything seems back to normal, you’ve successfully removed the malicious app!

Step 5: Consider Additional Security Measures

If you’ve had a run-in with malicious apps, you might want to take additional steps to secure your phone:

  • : Apps like Malwarebytes or Avast can help detect and remove malicious software from your phone.
  • Be Mindful of Permissions: Always review the permissions that apps request. If an app asks for more permissions than necessary, consider uninstalling it.
  • Download Apps Only from Trusted Sources: Stick to downloading apps from the Google Play Store, as they are generally safer than third-party stores.

Malicious apps can be sneaky, but with a little attention, you can usually track down and remove them with ease. By following these steps, you’ll be able to identify recently installed apps and remove the ones causing issues.

items.title: Breaking Language Barriers: Multilingual Content with Flotiq's Plugins and DeepL
items.author: Magdalena Sobantka
items.content:
Traditionally managing multilingual content meant creating separate content entries for each language. This is time-consuming and error-prone. Flotiq’s plugins tackle this head-on and provide a seamless experience for creating and managing multilingual content.

Breaking Down Language Barriers

In today’s digital world, your content, application or business isn’t limited to a single region - it can reach a global audience. Making your content available in multiple languages is no longer an advantage, it’s a necessity. Language should never be a barrier, whether you’re engaging with international customers, expanding your brand reach or ensuring smooth communication within a multilingual company.

With DeepL integrated into Flotiq, translating content has never been easier. Businesses can now provide accurate and context-aware translations and connect with diverse audiences worldwide. Whether you’re managing an intranet for a multinational company or publishing content for global users, you can ensure language differences don’t get in the way of clarity and engagement. By removing language barriers you enhance collaboration, prevent miscommunication and foster stronger international relationships - making your content go global.

Flotiq’s Multilingual plugin - a foundation for global content

Flotiq’s multilingual plugin provides a solid foundation for managing content in multiple languages. It allows you to define language variants for your content types so each piece of content is available in multiple languages.

Language Variant Management: Multiple Languages

  • The plugin allows you to associate language variants with your content types to have a structured approach to multilingual content.
  • This ensures content is organized and easily accessible in the desired languages. Managing language pairs is crucial for machine translation services and effective multilingual communication.

Intuitive Content Editing:

  • Content editors have a user-friendly interface that allows them to switch between language variants.
  • This simplifies the process of creating and editing content in multiple languages. High translation quality is key for good content editing.

DeepL Integration -  effortless translation

While the multilingual plugin provides the structure, the DeepL plugin adds the power of automated translation. DeepL, renowned for its accuracy, integrates seamlessly with Flotiq, allowing you to translate content with a single click.

One-Click translation: target language

  • Content editors can leverage DeepL to translate content directly within the Flotiq interface.
  • This eliminates the need for manual copy-pasting or external translation tools.

Maintaining consistency: translation quality

  • DeepL’s algorithms ensure consistency across language variants so your message remains clear and accurate. Consistency is key when you’re targeting a diverse audience - managing multilingual content in a headless CMS, localizing applications, or expanding your business globally.

Creating a multilingual content strategy

For businesses and organizations that operate globally, creating a multilingual content strategy is not just nice to have - it’s necessary. In the age of globalization, reaching your audience in their language can increase engagement and sales. A well-crafted multilingual content strategy allows you to reach a broader audience while respecting their cultural and linguistic preferences.

When developing this strategy, you need to consider the target language, cultural nuances, and local preferences. DeepL’s translation technology plays a significant role here, providing high-quality, accurate translations so your message resonates across languages. By using DeepL, you can create multilingual content that’s both linguistically accurate and culturally relevant, thus maximizing your global reach.

Quality nuances in multilingual content

Understanding and addressing quality nuances in multilingual content is crucial when creating content for a global audience. Each language has its own set of cultural nuances, idioms and expressions that can impact translation quality. DeepL’s neural machine translation technology is designed to consider these nuances and deliver accurate and culturally correct translations.

But remember, while machine translation has come a long way, it’s not infallible. Human review is still a critical step in ensuring the highest quality. Flotiq allows for human review and editing, so you can fine-tune translations and ensure they meet your quality standards. This combination of advanced technology and human oversight ensures your multilingual content is precise and impactful.

A Developer's perspective: simplifying multilingual implementation

Implementing multilingual support in a Headless CMS can often be a complex and time-consuming task for developers. It requires careful content structuring, API integrations, and translation management. A content creator's perspective

items.title: The Difference Between Risk Assessment and Risk Mitigation
items.author: CloudDefense.AI
items.content:
Effective cloud security hinges on two key processes: risk assessment and risk mitigation. Risk assessment involves identifying and evaluating security threats, while risk mitigation focuses on implementing protective measures to minimize those risks. Together, they create a proactive security framework that prevents breaches and ensures compliance.

Risk assessment is the process of identifying vulnerabilities, evaluating potential threats, and prioritizing security risks within cloud environments. It helps organizations understand where they are most exposed and take proactive steps before cybercriminals exploit weaknesses.

Common Cloud Security Risks:

  • Publicly exposed storage, weak security settings, and improper access permissions.
  • Software Vulnerabilities: Security flaws in cloud applications, virtual machines, and containerized environments.
  • Inadequate Access Controls: Overly broad identity and access management (IAM) policies.
  • Unprotected sensitive data, including personally identifiable information (PII).

Why Risk Assessment is Essential

Cloud environments are dynamic, constantly evolving with new workloads, configurations, and security challenges. Without continuous risk assessment, organizations risk overlooking critical vulnerabilities. Key challenges include multi-cloud complexity, cloud-native infrastructure, and shared responsibility security models.

How Organizations Perform Risk Assessments:

  • Automated Security Scanning: Detects vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
  • Evaluates cloud environments against regulatory frameworks such as SOC 2, HIPAA, and GDPR.
  • Threat Intelligence & Monitoring: Identifies suspicious activities and weak points in real-time. By integrating automated assessment tools, organizations can proactively detect and address security gaps before they become major threats.

Once risks are identified, risk mitigation ensures they are addressed through preventive measures, security controls, and automated fixes. The goal is to reduce the likelihood of cyberattacks and data breaches.

Effective Risk Mitigation Strategies:

  • Patching vulnerabilities and correcting misconfigurations.
  • Access Control Strengthening: Enforcing least privilege access to limit exposure.
  • Data Protection Measures: Encrypting sensitive data in transit and at rest.
  • Incident Response Planning: Establishing protocols for responding to security incidents.

Why Risk Mitigation is Critical

Cyber threats continue to evolve, with attackers using automation to exploit weak points. Organizations must be equally proactive in mitigating risks, rather than reacting after an incident.

Key drivers for risk mitigation include:

  • Preventing Automated Attacks: Hackers use bots to scan for vulnerabilities—automated remediation is needed to counteract these threats.
  • Ensuring Regulatory Compliance: Many industries impose strict security requirements, and failure to mitigate risks can lead to fines and legal consequences.
  • Reducing Attack Surfaces: By continuously applying security controls, businesses minimize entry points for potential attacks.

How Risk Mitigation is Implemented:

  • Automated Policy Enforcement: Ensuring security standards are applied consistently.
  • Detecting and responding to threats in real time.
  • Security Frameworks & Best Practices: Following cloud security guidelines to protect infrastructure and data.

Mitigation is an ongoing effort that requires constant updates to counteract new threats.

The Connection Between Risk Assessment and Risk Mitigation

Risk assessment identifies security gaps, while risk mitigation ensures those gaps are addressed. Both must work together to maintain a strong cloud security posture.

Why an Integrated Approach Matters:

  • Assessment without mitigation is ineffective—detecting risks but failing to address them leaves systems vulnerable.
  • Mitigation without assessment lacks focus—fixing low-priority issues while ignoring critical threats wastes resources.
  • A comprehensive security strategy requires continuous risk detection and proactive mitigation.

How They Complement Each Other:

  • Assessment pinpoints security weaknesses; mitigation applies fixes to prevent exploitation.
  • Continuous assessment allows real-time mitigation, keeping security controls up to date.
  • Automation reduces human error, ensuring risks are addressed as soon as they are detected.

By integrating both processes, businesses can stay ahead of cyber threats rather than merely reacting to them.

CloudDefense.AI: Unifying Risk Assessment and Mitigation

Managing cloud security requires both detection and response capabilities—CloudDefense.AI provides an automated, intelligent approach to securing cloud environments.

Key Capabilities of CloudDefense.AI:

  • Automated Threat Detection: Identifies security risks across cloud workloads and applications.
  • Provides continuous security insights to prevent cyber threats.
  • Identity & Access Management Oversight: Ensures IAM policies follow the least privilege model.
  • Instant Risk Remediation: Fixes misconfigurations and applies patches automatically.
  • Ensures adherence to SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, and other security frameworks.

Why Businesses Trust CloudDefense.AI:

  • Adapts to cloud environments of any size, from startups to enterprises.
  • Proactive Threat Prevention: Stops attacks before they cause harm.
  • Works across AWS, Azure, GCP, and multi-cloud infrastructures.

Organizations using CloudDefense.AI gain a unified security platform that not only detects risks but actively mitigates them.

Cloud security isn’t just about finding risks—it’s about eliminating them before they lead to breaches. Risk assessment helps organizations understand where they are vulnerable, while risk mitigation ensures proactive action is taken.

By integrating both processes through automated security solutions, businesses can strengthen their defenses, stay compliant, and reduce their exposure to cyber threats.

Want to secure your cloud environment effortlessly? Book a free demo with CloudDefense.AI and take charge of your risk management strategy today!

Understanding Laravel Telescope and Its Challenges

Laravel Telescope is a powerful debugging and monitoring tool for Laravel applications. It provides deep insights into queries, jobs, and requests, making development more efficient. However, using Telescope extensively during development or handling high-intensity jobs and queries can lead to rapid database growth.

A major problem is that the table can become bloated, sometimes reaching dozens of gigabytes.

Laravel's Built-in Pruning and Clearing Methods

Laravel provides built-in commands to manage Telescope's database storage:

  • – This command removes old entries run by scheduler.
  • – This command deletes all Telescope records.

However, has several downsides:

Performance Issues with

The command executes a statement on the table, which can take extremely long time if the table is large. Here's the internal implementation of the command:

MySQL Storage Retention Issue

Even after a successful , MySQL does not immediately release the space occupied by deleted rows. This is because InnoDB does not shrink the file size automatically, it only marks space as available for future inserts. This means that the table will continue to consume excessive storage unless manually optimized.

Introducing Laravel-Telescope-Flusher

  • Performing fast deletions using the command.
  • with MySQL's .

Efficient Clearing with Laravel-Telescope-Flusher

Here's the core implementation of the package:

  • : is significantly faster than because it resets the table and does not generate large transaction logs.
  • : The command ensures that MySQL reclaims the disk space previously occupied by deleted records.

If you are experiencing large tables slowing down your development or consuming excessive storage, laravel-telescope-flusher is an excellent solution. It provides a quick, efficient way to manage Telescope entries without performance bottlenecks.

items.title: How To Build An Interactive Quiz Project Using Vanilla JavaScript
items.author: Emmanuel C. Okolie
items.content:
One fun thing about JavaScript is that it can be used to do a lot of mind-blowing things. Now, for educational purposes, I’ve always longed for an Interactive Quiz Project that beginners can easily follow.

So for this Guide, we’ll be looking at how To Build An Interactive Quiz Project.

After following the step-by-step guide in this tutorial, the Interactive Quiz Project will be up and running in your browser interface. Without wasting much of your time, let’s jump into this tutorial.

To achieve the Interactive Quiz Project, you should be familiar with the following languages:

Step-by-Step Process Of Making An Interactive Quiz Session

So, we’re about to dive into the process of making a quiz session for this project! Note. We’ll be using basic arithmetic as our example.

Without further delay, let’s head on. First, you’ll create a folder. In our case, we’re calling it Then you’ll create a file inside of this folder and name it Then you’ll open it in your code editor.

so the index.html will contain codes like this

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
        <meta charset="utf-8">
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
        <title>Quiz Tutorial</title>
        <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="style.css">
</head>
<body>
        <div class="center">
                <div id="quiz"></div>
                <button id="submit">Get Results</button>
                <div id="results"></div>
        </div>

        <script type="text/javascript" src="main.js"></script>

</body>
</html>

After doing this, your interface will look something like this 👇

Once you get to this point, you know you’re on the right track. And we need to add some styling to it. To make it presentable, create another file called and add the following codes inside it.

body{
        font-size: 20px;
        font-family: sans-serif;
        color: #333;
}
.center{
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    justify-content: center;
    align-items: center;
    height: 100vh; /* Adjust as needed */
}

.question{
        font-weight: 600;
}
.answers {
    margin-bottom: 20px;
}
#submit{
        font-family: sans-serif;
        font-size: 20px;
        background-color: #297;
        color: #fff;
        border: 0px;
        border-radius: 3px;
        padding: 20px;
        cursor: pointer;
        margin-bottom: 20px;
}
#submit:hover{
        background-color: #3a8;
}

After doing this, the interface will change to something like this: 👇

If you notice, the aritimatics are not showing, and it wasn’t added in the HTML. Good, that is because we’re fetching the maths questions from our JavaScript. To see how we did that, create another file and name it as and add the codes below

var quizContainer = document.getElementById('quiz');
var resultsContainer = document.getElementById('results');
var submitButton = document.getElementById('submit');


var myQuestions = [
        {
                question: "What is 10/2?",
                answers: {
                        a: '3',
                        b: '5',
                        c: '115'
                },
                correctAnswer: 'b'
        },
        {
                question: "What is 30/3?",
                answers: {
                        a: '3',
                        b: '5',
                        c: '10'
                },
                correctAnswer: 'c'
        }
];


function generateQuiz(questions, quizContainer, resultsContainer, submitButton){

        function showQuestions(questions, quizContainer){
                // code will go here
                // we'll need a place to store the output and the answer choices
                var output = [];
                var answers;

                // for each question...
                for(var i=0; i<questions.length; i++){

                        // first reset the list of answers
                        answers = [];

                        // for each available answer to this question...
                        for(letter in questions[i].answers){

                                // ...add an html radio button
                                answers.push(
                                        '<label>'
                                                + '<input type="radio" name="question'+i+'" value="'+letter+'">'
                                                + letter + ': '
                                                + questions[i].answers[letter]
                                        + '</label>'
                                );
                        }

                        // add this question and its answers to the output
                        output.push(
                                '<div class="question">' + questions[i].question + '</div>'
                                + '<div class="answers">' + answers.join('') + '</div>'
                        );
                }

                // finally combine our output list into one string of html and put it on the page
                quizContainer.innerHTML = output.join('');
        }

        function showResults(questions, quizContainer, resultsContainer){
                // code will go here


                // gather answer containers from our quiz
                var answerContainers = quizContainer.querySelectorAll('.answers');

                // keep track of user's answers
                var userAnswer = '';
                var numCorrect = 0;

                // for each question...
                for(var i=0; i<questions.length; i++){

                        // find selected answer
                        userAnswer = (answerContainers[i].querySelector('input[name=question'+i+']:checked')||{}).value;

                        // if answer is correct
                        if(userAnswer===questions[i].correctAnswer){
                                // add to the number of correct answers
                                numCorrect++;

                                // color the answers green
                                answerContainers[i].style.color = 'lightgreen';
                        }
                        // if answer is wrong or blank
                        else{
                                // color the answers red
                                answerContainers[i].style.color = 'red';
                        }
                }

                // show number of correct answers out of total
                resultsContainer.innerHTML = numCorrect + ' out of ' + questions.length;
        }

        // show the questions
        showQuestions(questions, quizContainer);

        // when user clicks submit, show results
        submitButton.onclick = function(){
                showResults(questions, quizContainer, resultsContainer);
                // on submit, show results
        }
}

generateQuiz(myQuestions, quizContainer, resultsContainer, submitButton);

The above code makes the math question display and also display the result once you click . Here’s a GIF that shows your end result: 👇

Building an interactive quiz project using Vanilla JavaScript is an exciting way to enhance your web development skills while creating a dynamic and engaging user experience. By following a step-by-step approach, you can break down the project into manageable tasks, such as structuring the HTML, styling with CSS, and implementing JavaScript functionalities for question handling, answer validation, and score tracking.

Through this process, you gain hands-on experience with JavaScript concepts like event listeners, DOM manipulation, and data handling, which are crucial for interactive web applications. Additionally, you can enhance the quiz by adding features such as timers, progress tracking, and animations to make it more engaging.

With practice and creativity, you can expand your quiz project into a more sophisticated application, integrating APIs or databases for a richer experience. The key takeaway is that Vanilla JavaScript provides a solid foundation for interactive web development, empowering you to build projects that are both functional and user-friendly. Keep experimenting, and you'll continue to improve your skills as a JavaScript developer! 🚀

Full-stack Laravel developer has years of experience in software development. He has developed full-fledged skills by combining software development, writing, and instructing others in what he does.

His stacks include and other languages and frameworks. As a freelancer, he creates websites for clients and writes technical guides to show people how to do what he does.

items.title: Tender Management System: Everything You Need to Know in 2025
items.author: Bhavesh Gangani
items.content:
In today’s competitive business landscape, managing tenders effectively is crucial for organizations looking to secure contracts and streamline procurement processes. A tender management system helps businesses automate and organize the entire tendering lifecycle, from bid creation to contract awarding.

Traditional tendering methods often involve excessive paperwork, manual data entry, and communication gaps, leading to inefficiencies and missed opportunities. With a digital tender management solution, businesses can simplify the bidding process, ensure compliance, and improve decision-making through data-driven insights.

Whether you’re a contractor, supplier, or procurement manager, understanding how a tender management system works and its key features can help you enhance efficiency and increase your chances of winning tenders. This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about tender management software in 2025.

What is a Tender Management System?

A tender management system is a digital solution that automates and streamlines the tendering process for businesses, government agencies, and procurement teams. It helps in creating, managing, and tracking tender documents while ensuring compliance with industry regulations.

This system eliminates manual inefficiencies by providing a centralized platform where businesses can handle bid submissions, evaluate vendor proposals, and award contracts seamlessly.

Whether you’re handling government tenders, construction bids, or corporate procurement, a tender management software ensures a smooth and transparent process.

Key Features of a Tender Management System

When selecting the right tender management software, look for these essential features:

1. Automated Tender Creation

A tender management system enables businesses to generate structured tender documents automatically, reducing human errors and ensuring accuracy.

2. Bid Submission & Tracking

Vendors can submit bids through an online portal, while procurement teams can track the progress of each tender in real time.

All tender-related documents, including RFQs (Request for Quotations), RFPs (Request for Proposals), and contracts, are securely stored in a centralized database.

4. Evaluation & Scoring System

An intelligent scoring mechanism allows businesses to evaluate bids based on predefined criteria, ensuring fair and transparent vendor selection.

The system automates approval workflows, notifications, and deadlines, ensuring that tenders move through different stages efficiently.

6. Compliance & Audit Trails

A tender management system helps organizations maintain compliance with legal and industry-specific regulations while keeping an audit trail of all transactions.

7. Integration with ERP & Other Tools

Seamless integration with ERP solution, CRM software, and financial management tools enhances collaboration and decision-making.

Benefits of Using a Tender Management System

Adopting a tender management system provides several advantages, including:

– Reduces manual paperwork and automates repetitive tasks, saving time and effort.

– Ensures a fair evaluation process and eliminates the risk of biased decision-making.

– Helps organizations adhere to regulatory requirements and industry best practices.

– Data-driven insights and automated bid evaluations accelerate procurement decisions.

– Reduces administrative costs by digitizing the entire tendering process.

Choosing the Right Tender Management System in 2025

With multiple tender management software options available, selecting the right one depends on your business needs. Consider the following factors:

– Choose a solution that caters to your industry-specific tendering requirements.

Customization & Scalability – Ensure the software can adapt to changing business needs and scale as your organization grows.

– A well-designed interface makes it easy for users to navigate and operate.

– Look for features like data encryption and role-based access control to maintain security and confidentiality.

– Select a system that integrates with project management, auto parts management system, and other enterprise tools.

A tender management system is a game-changer for businesses looking to streamline their procurement and bidding processes. By leveraging an advanced tender management software, organizations can enhance efficiency, reduce risks, and improve transparency in the tendering lifecycle.

items.title: Machine Learning Tutorial: Learn ML with Real-World Projects
items.author: Machine Learning
items.content:
In today’s technology-driven world, machine learning (ML) has become one of the most sought-after skills. Whether you’re a student, a professional, or an enthusiast, learning machine learning through real-world projects is the best way to gain practical experience. This machine learning tutorial for beginners will guide you through the basics of ML and show you how to implement real-world projects to enhance your learning.

What is Machine Learning?

Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence (AI) that enables computers to learn from data without explicit programming. It involves training models to recognize patterns and make predictions. ML is widely used in various fields, such as healthcare, finance, e-commerce, and autonomous vehicles.

Types of Machine Learning

The model learns from labeled data, making predictions based on input-output pairs.

2.Unsupervised Learning – The model finds hidden patterns in unlabeled data.

3.Reinforcement Learning – The model learns through trial and error, receiving rewards for positive actions.

Why Learn Machine Learning Through Real-World Projects?

Many beginners struggle with theoretical concepts. Applying machine learning tutorial knowledge to real-world projects helps you:

· Understand how ML algorithms work.

· Gain hands-on experience with datasets.

· Develop problem-solving skills.

· Build a portfolio to showcase your skills.

Essential Tools and Libraries for Machine Learning

Before diving into projects, familiarize yourself with popular ML tools: · The most popular language for ML.

· For data manipulation.

· For data visualization.

· For implementing ML algorithms.

· For deep learning projects.

Real-World Machine Learning Projects

Here are three real-world projects that will help you apply your machine learning tutorial for beginners knowledge:

1. Predicting House Prices

Problem: House prices depend on factors like location, size, and amenities. Predicting prices accurately is crucial for real estate businesses.

1.Collect historical housing data (e.g., Kaggle datasets).

2.Perform data cleaning and visualization.

3.Use regression models like Linear Regression or Random Forest.

4.Evaluate the model using metrics like Mean Absolute Error (MAE). Regression modeling and feature engineering.

2. Sentiment Analysis on Customer Reviews

Problem: Businesses want to analyze customer feedback to improve products and services.

1.Gather review data from sources like Amazon, Yelp, or Twitter.

2.Preprocess text data (tokenization, removing stop words, stemming).

3.Use NLP techniques and ML models (Naïve Bayes, LSTM, or BERT) for classification.

4.Evaluate model accuracy using precision, recall, and F1-score.

Natural Language Processing (NLP) and text classification.

3.Fraud Detection in Transactions

Problem: Financial institutions need to detect fraudulent transactions to prevent losses.

1.Obtain a dataset of transaction history with fraud labels.

2.Analyze and preprocess the data (handle missing values, normalize data).

3.Train models like Logistic Regression, Random Forest, or Neural Networks.

4.Evaluate model performance using confusion matrix and ROC curve.

: Classification algorithms and anomaly detection.

Tips for Learning Machine Learning Effectively

  1. Start with Basics: Master Python, statistics, and basic ML concepts before moving to advanced topics.

2.Practice with Datasets: Use platforms like Kaggle, UCI Machine Learning Repository, and Google Dataset Search.

3.Work on Projects: The best way to learn ML is by implementing projects.

4.Join Communities: Participate in forums like Stack Overflow, GitHub, and ML Meetups.

5.Keep Learning: Follow ML blogs, research papers, and online courses.

This machine learning tutorial introduced ML fundamentals and real-world projects to help beginners gain practical experience. By working on projects like house price prediction, sentiment analysis, and fraud detection, you’ll develop essential ML skills and build a strong portfolio. Keep experimenting with different datasets and algorithms to improve your understanding. Happy learning!

items.title: How to Setup Nomba Checkout on shopify
items.author: Makanju Oluwafemi
items.content:
Nomba provides a Shopify checkout plugin to accept payment in your online store. This plugin is similar to the WordPress plugin simply because they are both used for accepting online payments. Also, it belongs to the no-code ecosystem. When building a Shopify store, there are different ways to accept payment.

Typically, payment is a third-party thing for Shopify, but then they support some providers by default. This process is slightly different when you want to use Nomba as a payment provider in your Shopify store.

This Plugin is available to Nigerian merchants and it can be used to process internatio nal and local Payments.

To use Nomba as your Shopify store payment provider, you will need to on the Nomba Website > go to the settings page on your dashboard. Click on Get . The process is straightforward.

These are the 3 things to get on the settings page before adding Nomba as a payment provider on your store. We provide the option to switch between live and test keys.

    Step-by-step installation guide

    The step requires that you add Nomba as a third-party app. Look at Nomba Shopify plugin here and click on Install When you click on Install, you will be redirected to your Shopify admin page, where you can now properly install the Nomba app. Click on Install to continue.

    When you successfully install this app, You will be redirected to Nomba payment gateway config page. On this page, You will add your Client ID, private secret, and account ID.

    You can get test and live keys on your settings page when you have completed setting up your dashboard.

    Click on the Ready checkbox, then click the Submit button, and you will get a notification that your data was saved without issue. Click go back to payment settings to activate the Nomba app.

    This image shows a notification update, describing the status of the action you just completed.

    Click on the arrow left button and you will be redirected to a page where you can now activate Nomba for your Shopify app.

    Click on the activate button and you have successfully enabled Nomba as a payment service in your Shopify App. You can also choose to select which card you would love your business to accept but for this tutorial we have all selected.

    With Nomba, you gain access to a reliable and secure payment solution that enhances your store’s checkout experience. Whether you're running a small business or scaling an enterprise, using Nomba as a payment provider, ensures that your customers enjoy smooth payment processing experience. Now, your business can thrive without thinking of any payment hassle.

items.title: AI Revolutionizing Amsterdam’s Web Design: Innovation, Efficiency & Creativity
items.author: dev
items.content:
Amsterdam has long been at the forefront of digital innovation, blending creativity with technology to produce world-class web design. In recent years, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become a driving force in reshaping how web design is approached, implemented, and optimized. From automating repetitive tasks to enhancing user experience and personalization, AI is revolutionizing the industry in Amsterdam, making websites smarter, faster, and more intuitive. This article explores the role of AI in Amsterdam’s web design industry, highlighting its impact on efficiency, creativity, and user engagement.

AI-Powered Web Design: A Game Changer

AI is transforming web design by enabling designers and developers to automate processes, enhance user experience, and analyze data-driven insights. and tech startups are increasingly integrating AI into their workflows to streamline operations and improve performance.

  1. Automated Web Development

One of the most significant contributions of AI to web design is automation. AI-powered tools like Wix ADI, Framer AI, and Webflow utilize machine learning algorithms to generate website layouts, optimize content placement, and even suggest design improvements.

Key Benefits of AI in Web Development:

Time Efficiency: AI speeds up the design and development process by automating repetitive coding tasks.

Cost Reduction: Small businesses and startups in Amsterdam benefit from AI-driven website builders that reduce the need for large development teams.

Template Optimization: AI suggests the best UI/UX elements based on industry trends and user preferences.

By leveraging automation, Amsterdam-based web designers can focus more on creativity and user experience rather than spending time on manual coding.

  1. AI-Driven Personalization

User experience is at the heart of modern web design, and AI plays a crucial role in delivering personalized content. Amsterdam’s web design agencies integrate AI-driven recommendation engines to customize website content based on user behavior, preferences, and demographics.

How AI Enhances Personalization:

Dynamic Content Generation: AI analyzes user data to display personalized content in real time.

Chatbots & Virtual Assistants: Many Amsterdam-based businesses use AI chatbots to provide instant customer support and guide users through websites.

Behavioral Analytics: AI tracks user interactions to recommend products, services, or articles based on browsing history.

Personalization powered by AI creates a more engaging and customized experience, leading to higher user satisfaction and conversion rates.

User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design are crucial elements of modern web design. AI helps designers in Amsterdam analyze vast amounts of data to optimize UI/UX elements and improve usability.

Key AI Applications in UX/UI:

A/B Testing Automation: AI conducts A/B testing at scale, identifying which design elements perform best.

Heatmap Analysis: AI-powered tools like Hotjar and Crazy Egg track user interactions to optimize website layouts.

Voice & Gesture Recognition: AI-driven interfaces enable voice commands and touchless navigation, improving accessibility.

Amsterdam’s leading digital agencies utilize AI insights to craft websites that are both aesthetically pleasing and highly functional.

  1. SEO & AI-Driven Content Optimization

Search Engine Optimization (SEO) is essential for improving website visibility. AI-powered tools help Amsterdam-based web designers optimize websites for better search engine rankings by analyzing keywords, metadata, and content structure.

Content Optimization: AI tools like Clearscope and Surfer SEO suggest keyword-rich content to improve rankings.

Automated Meta Tags & Descriptions: AI generates SEO-friendly meta descriptions and alt texts for images.

Voice Search Optimization: AI assists in structuring content for voice search, aligning with evolving user behaviors.

AI ensures that websites are not only visually appealing but also optimized for search engines, driving organic traffic to businesses.

  1. AI & Security in Web Design

Cybersecurity is a growing concern in the digital landscape. AI-powered security solutions help Amsterdam’s web designers and developers enhance website security by detecting vulnerabilities and mitigating risks.

AI-Powered Security Features:

Fraud Detection: AI identifies suspicious user behavior and prevents fraudulent activities.

Automated Threat Detection: AI-driven security systems scan for vulnerabilities in real-time.

Data Encryption & Compliance: AI ensures websites comply with GDPR and other data protection regulations.

With AI-enhanced security, Amsterdam-based web designers can build safer, more reliable digital experiences.

  1. AI & Sustainable Web Design

Sustainability is a growing focus in Amsterdam’s tech scene. AI contributes to eco-friendly web design by optimizing energy consumption and reducing carbon footprints.

Sustainable Web Practices with AI:

Efficient Coding Practices: AI minimizes redundant code, leading to faster-loading pages and lower energy usage.

Image & Video Compression: AI optimizes media files to reduce bandwidth consumption.

Green Hosting Recommendations: AI suggests environmentally friendly web hosting services that align with Amsterdam’s sustainability goals.

AI-driven sustainability initiatives align with Amsterdam’s commitment to digital innovation and environmental consciousness.

The Future of AI in Amsterdam’s Web Design Industry

As AI continues to evolve, its impact on web design in Amsterdam will only grow. The future holds exciting possibilities, including:

AI-Generated Creative Design: AI tools capable of creating complex, artistic layouts tailored to brand identities.

Hyper-Personalized Web Experiences: AI refining personalization strategies for even deeper user engagement.

Voice & AI-Driven Web Navigation: Enhancing accessibility through natural language processing and voice recognition.

Amsterdam’s web design industry is set to remain at the forefront of AI innovation, embracing cutting-edge technologies to create user-centric, sustainable, and intelligent web experiences.

AI is revolutionizing Amsterdam’s web design industry by enhancing efficiency, personalization, security, and sustainability. With AI-powered automation, data-driven insights, and advanced UX/UI capabilities, web designers in Amsterdam are crafting digital experiences that are both functional and engaging. As AI continues to advance, its role in shaping the future of web design will only expand, ensuring that Amsterdam remains a global leader in digital innovation.

items.title: Angular Technical Debt: Types and Mitigation Strategies
items.author: Nicholas Jones
items.content:
One of the toughest things in the software development lifecycle is to tackle technical debt, especially in dynamic and scalable frameworks like Angular. As the Angular applications evolve, rapid development cycles, shortcuts, and legacy code can accumulate technical debt which leads to performance issues, poor maintainability, and slower development in the future. In this article, we will explore the most common types of Angular technical debt and effective strategies to reduce and manage them.

Technical debt in a software development project refers to the accumulation of code quality issues, design compromises or shortcuts that are taken during the development which might lead to increased maintenance costs and difficulties in future updates. This type of Angular technical debt arises due to focusing on rapid delivery rather than focusing on well-strucutred code.

Most Common Types of Angular Technical Debt

The following are some of the most common types of Angular technical debt that you should take care of.

1. Outdated Angular Versions

Delaying the Angular version upgrades creates technical debt by encouraging incompatibility with current libraries and tools which exposes projects to security vulnerabilities, and hindering the adoption of beneficial new Angular features, ultimately slowing development and increasing maintenance burdens.

2. Poor Component Architecture

It is one of those Angular technical debts that often leads to overly large components that handle excessive logic, which result in coupling between components and limited resusability of services and directives. This typically happens due to rushed development, where components exceed hundreds of lines, combining both presentation and business logic which makes the codebase difficult to maintain, test, and scale.

3. Inefficient State Management

This type of technical debt arises in Angular applications when there is no standardized approach. It leads development teams to rely on component-level state, custom services, or third-party libraries like NgRx or Akita. Without clear guidelines, state logic becomes unorganized across components and services which results in unmanageable code, complex debugging, and challenging refactoring in large-scale applications.

4. Inconsistent Coding Standards

Inconsistent standard of coding is one of the Angular technical debt that occurs in large teams when coding conventions are not used. It results in differences in naming conventions, inconsistent use of RxJS patterns, and mixed approaches to dependency injection, observables, and change detection strategies. This lack of uniformity reduces code readability, complicates maintenance of applications, and increasaes the hiring time of new Angular developers.

5. Lack of Adequate Testing

Due to tight deadlines, the adequate testing in Angular projects often arises which might lead to low unit test coverage for components and services, neglected end-to-end tests, and brittle tests that easily break with minor changes. This insufficient testing increases the risk of regression which makes the future development slower and more error prone.

6. Unoptimized RxJS Utilization

The unoptimized RxJS often lead to technical debt due to unhandled subscriptions causing memory leaks, chained or deeply nested observables, creating callback hell and incorrect use of operators results in performance bottlenecks. Poor RxJS implementation can significantly impact application performance and maintainability.

How to Reduce Angular Technical Debt?

The following are the ways to reduce the technical debt in Angular applications.

1. Keep Angular and Dependencies Updated:

Regularly update Angular version and its dependencies using various tools like ng update to stay aligned with the latest major and minor releases. This helps to minimize technical debt by addressing security patches, performance improvements, and new features incrementally.

2. Follow Component Best Practices:

You should try to follow the Single Responsibility Principle by breaking down large components into smaller, more focused units which will help to improve maintainability and resuability. Additionally, employing container-presentational patterns can help you in enhancing the separation of concerns which leads to cleaner code and a more organized application structure.

3. Adopt a Clear State Management Strategy:

Choose an appropriate state management approach based on project size - local state, service-based state, or centralized state management like NgRx or Akita. Also you need document state handling guidelines to maintain consistency across the team.

4. Enforce Coding Standards

Use tools like ESLint and Prettier to standardize code formatting and enforce coding conventions. Try to establish a shared style guide and conduct regular code reviews to ensure consistent coding practices.

5. Invest in Comprehensive Testing

Build a strong testing culture with mandatory unit, integration, and end-to-end tests. Aim for a minimum of 80% test coverage and leverage Angular’s TestBed for effective unit testing which try to reduce the chances of regressions.

6. Externalize Configurations

Manage environment-specific settings using environment files and implement configurations from APIs or remote configurations services which ensures seamless deployment across different environments.

7. Perform Regular Code Audits

Conduct regular code audits using tools like SonarQube to identify code quality issues, technical debt, and potential bugs. Maintain a technical debt backlog and prioritize high-risk items for resolution, which ensure long-term code health.

Angular technical debt is inevitable, but with proactive strategies and consistent coding practices, you can minimize its impact and ensure long-term maintainability. By keeping Angular updated, enforcing standards, optimizing state management, and improving testing culture, teams can keep a balance between fast delivery and sustainable code quality. Additionally, partnering with an Angular development company can help you optimize your application’s architecture, reduce technical debt, and accelerate development while maintaining code quality. By proactively managing technical debt you will be able to enhance the development process by ensuring that your Angular application remains future-proof and scalable.

items.title: Virtual vs Augmented: A Tech Comparison
items.author: Anaya Tanha
items.content:
Technology is moving faster than ever, and two of the most exciting innovations today are Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR). It’s like stepping into a whole new world, where you can experience things in a way that feels almost unreal. But here’s the thing — many people still get confused between AR and VR.

Let me break it down for you in the simplest and most exciting way possible. Plus, I’ll show you how both technologies are transforming , content marketing, social media marketing, and even email marketing.

Understanding Virtual Reality (VR)

Alright, let me start with Virtual Reality (VR). Imagine putting on a headset and suddenly finding yourself on a beach in the Maldives or inside a video game where you are the main character. That’s VR for you!

Virtual Reality creates a completely immersive experience. It takes you away from your current surroundings and places you in a different, simulated world. It’s widely used in gaming, training simulations, and even virtual tourism.

Businesses are also using VR to boost their content marketing and social media marketing strategies. Pairing VR experiences with email marketing can drive massive engagement. If you want to transform your digital marketing strategies, FlashyMinds is an expert in helping businesses adapt to such modern trends.

Understanding Augmented Reality (AR)

Now, let’s talk about Augmented Reality (AR). AR is like adding a layer of virtual elements to the real world. Remember those Instagram filters that make you look like a cat or add makeup to your face? That’s AR in action.

AR doesn’t take you to another world; it enhances the world around you. Many businesses use AR to allow customers to visualize products like furniture, clothes, or makeup before purchasing them.

Combining AR with content marketing, social media marketing, and email marketing can significantly boost customer engagement. FlashyMinds does an incredible job of helping businesses make the most of AR in their marketing strategies.

Experience Comparison: VR vs AR

So, how do these two experiences feel different? Let me explain.

Virtual Reality (VR): It isolates you from the real world and takes you into a completely digital environment. You need special VR headsets like Oculus, PlayStation VR, or HTC Vive to experience this.

Augmented Reality (AR): It blends virtual elements with the real world. You don’t need a headset — your smartphone camera is enough to enjoy AR experiences.

Businesses are now investing heavily in content marketing and strategies that integrate VR/AR experiences to increase customer engagement. FlashyMinds, for example, is already helping brands bridge this gap and drive higher conversions.

How AR and VR Boost Digital Marketing

Here’s where things get interesting. AR and VR are changing the entire game of Digital Marketing Services. Companies are now creating immersive experiences to attract and retain customers.

Content marketing: Brands create VR stories or AR filters to engage users.

The best part? FlashyMinds helps brands integrate these strategies seamlessly. They understand how to maximize engagement through modern technology.

Practical Use Cases of VR

You might wonder, where exactly is VR being used today? Here’s a quick look:

Gaming: Fully immersive games where players feel like they are inside the game.

Education: Virtual classrooms where students can experience learning beyond books.

Healthcare: Virtual surgeries for medical training.

Real estate: Virtual house tours to help buyers visualize properties.

Businesses that integrate VR with content marketing and social media marketing often see higher conversion rates. FlashyMinds can help businesses tap into this trend with the right marketing strategies.

Practical Use Cases of AR

AR is literally transforming the way we shop, learn, and experience things in real life. Some practical use cases of AR include:

E-commerce: Virtual try-ons for clothes, makeup, or home decor.

Social media: Fun and interactive AR filters to boost engagement.

Healthcare: AR-assisted surgeries and medical learning.

Travel: AR-guided tours of historical places.

When businesses combine AR with SEO, email marketing, and content marketing, they see tremendous growth. FlashyMinds has been a leader in helping brands build impactful marketing strategies using AR.

AR vs VR: Which One is More Useful?

Honestly, both AR and VR are powerful. But their usage depends on the type of experience a business wants to offer.

For immersive experiences: VR is the winner. It’s perfect for gaming, virtual tours, and high-engagement experiences.

For practical applications: AR takes the lead. It’s perfect for enhancing customer experiences in e-commerce, healthcare, and social media marketing.

Businesses that strategically use both technologies with content marketing and social media marketing see game-changing results. And FlashyMinds can make that happen for any business.

Revolutionizing Business with FlashyMinds

So, if you’re planning to leverage AR and VR for your business — don’t think twice. Connect with FlashyMinds and watch how they transform your brand’s future.

items.title: Upgrading Odoo Expensive or Cheaper?
items.author: WebbyCrown Solutions
items.content:
Odoo, the open-source ERP software, runs businesses worldwide with its modular design and powerful features. Whether you’re managing inventory, sales or accounting, Odoo has a solution for you. But one question bothers many users: is upgrading Odoo expensive or cheap? The answer is not straightforward it depends on your setup, hosting and whether you go solo or partner with someone. In this blog post we’ll cover the nitty gritty of upgrading Odoo, address common concerns and give you practical tips to help you decide. We’ll break it down into three sections: cost and time, DIY versus partner and strategies to optimize your investment. Let’s get started!

Cost and Time of Upgrading Odoo

Why Upgrading Odoo is So Expensive and Time Consuming?

Upgrading Odoo can feel like a financial and logistical marathon for some businesses. The process involves migrating data, adapting custom modules and testing workflows each step adding to the bill. For example a company on Odoo Enterprise V15 with 24 users and minimal customization might get a quote of 272 hours from a partner, which translates to thousands of dollars. Why? Customizations, even small ones, require rework to align with the new version’s codebase. Partners also factor in testing and troubleshooting to ensure nothing breaks post upgrade.

Odoo upgrades involve migrating data, updating custom modules and testing compatibility with new features.

This process can be costly because of:

Customization and Integration Costs: If your business relies on custom Odoo modules, upgrading requires rewriting or adapting these which can range from $10,000 to $40,000.

Testing and Quality Assurance: Ensuring the new version works with your business processes takes time and resources, adding to the cost.

Data Migration: Migrating data from an older version to a newer one can be complex and costs vary from $0 to $20,000 depending on data complexity.

How Long Should an Odoo Upgrade Really Take?

Time varies greatly depending on complexity. A simple upgrade say from V16 to V17 with no custom modules might take 2-3 days if hosted on Odoo.sh or SaaS thanks to automated scripts and staging environments. But a heavily customised setup could take weeks. A Swiss company upgrading from V15 to V17, their partner quoted 40 hours but the actual migration took 4 hours after testing. Moral of the story? Minimal customisation and proper preparation shrink the timeline significantly.

How Does Odoo Hosting Impact Upgrades and Costs?

Your Odoo hosting Odoo.sh, Odoo Online or on-premise plays a big role. Odoo.sh makes upgrades easy with staging branches for testing, often making it a “walk in the park” for users who’ve used it since 2019. Odoo Online offers free hosting up to 100GB and includes upgrades in their plans but doesn’t support custom modules. On-premise hosting is cheaper upfront (e.g. via a VPS) but demands manual maintenance and expertise which can add up in costs over time. Hosting impacts the upgrade process and long term expenses so choose wisely.

DIY Upgrades vs Partner Support

Can I Upgrade Odoo Myself or Do I Need a Partner?

You can upgrade Odoo yourself if you’re tech savvy and your setup is standard. Odoo provides free upgrade scripts at upgrade.odoo.com upload your database, wait a few hours and get a migrated version back. A small business upgrading from V14 to V15 did this without a partner and found it “easy” but warned that complex changes could complicate things. Partners however bring expertise to handle “gotchas” like broken customizations or third party module conflicts, saving you headaches. For example a Reddit user noted partners have scripts to speed up the process, justifying their cost.

Why Do Odoo Partners Charge So Much for Support and Upgrades?

Partners don’t come cheap quotes can range from $40/hour to $3,500/month for implementation and upgrades. They charge for their expertise, tools and the assurance that your odoo system won’t crash mid-migration. A Gold Partner might charge $14,000 for a four-month odoo project, covering analysis, configuration and training. Critics call it a “scam” but for businesses with unique needs (e.g. integrating Swiss payroll in V17) this expertise prevents expensive errors. The trade off? You pay for peace of mind and efficiency.

What to Expect When Upgrading Odoo?

Expect hiccups like data migration glitches, custom module incompatibilities and user retraining. A company upgrading to V17 faced “minor issues” with custom processes, required partner tweaks. New users might also find odoo’s feature rich interface overwhelming adding a learning curve. Testing is critical skip it and you risk broken workflows. Staging environments (like odoo.sh) help but don’t eliminate the need for thorough checks.

Maximizing Value and Minimizing Costs

What are the Hidden Costs of Using Odoo?

Beyond licensing ($24.90/user/month for Standard, $37.40 for Custom) there are hidden costs. Implementation can go past 200 hours for complex setups and data migration from legacy erp systems adds to the bill. On-premise hosting requires server maintenance while odoo.sh or cloud plans bundle maintenance but scale with users. Support costs also add up Success Packs offer dedicated help but expire after a year. A small business paying $400/month still saves compared to NetSuite’s $3,000 but these extras can catch you off guard.

Is Odoo Worth it for Small Business?

For small business odoo shines. The Community edition is free and offers core apps like CRM and Sales while Enterprise’s One App Free plan suits tiny teams. A gas station owner found odoo’s $400/month fee “a bargain” compared to SAP’s pricier alternatives, cutting labor costs by over 8 hours weekly. Compared to Shopify or NetSuite odoo’s all in one approach delivers value if you leverage its integrations and avoid over customization.

Community vs Enterprise Edition: Which One to Choose?

odoo Community Edition is free and ideal for small business with basic functionality.

odoo Enterprise Edition offers advanced features, more customization options and dedicated support.It depends on your business needs. If you need advanced modules like accounting and manufacturing, odoo Enterprise Edition is the better option.

How Hosting Choices Affect Upgrade Costs

odoo offers different hosting options each impacting upgrade costs:

odoo Online (Cloud-Based): Automatic upgrades but limited customization.

odoo On-Premise: More control and flexibility but requires IT maintenance.

odoo SH (odoo Cloud Hosting): A balance between flexibility and support.

Choosing the right hosting option can reduce long term costs and simplify upgrades.

How to Reduce the Cost of Running and Upgrading odoo?

Cut costs by sticking to standard modules customizations increase upgrade bills. Host on odoo Online for free upgrades and no maintenance hassles or use odoo.sh for scalable testing. Self-hosting on a VPS cuts subscription fees but requires time. Use the Community edition or OCA modules for free alternatives to Enterprise add-ons. Test upgrades on a staging server first to catch issues early and avoid expensive fixes later.

What’s the difference between odoo Community and Enterprise Editions?

Community is free, open-source and limited to basic odoo apps, ideal for DIY enthusiasts. Enterprise adds premium odoo apps (e.g. Accounting, Manufacturing), cloud hosting and support for $24.90-$37.40/user/month. A small business might thrive on Community, while a growing business with 50+ users needs Enterprise’s scalability and polish. Upgrades are free for both via odoo’s scripts but Enterprise includes partner-grade support.

Take Control of your odoo Upgrade Today

Upgrading odoo can be costly or cheaper it depends on your approach. Heavy customizations and partner reliance increase costs but standard setups and DIY efforts keep costs low. Time-wise a simple upgrade takes days not weeks especially with the right hosting. Small business can enjoy odoo’s benefits without breaking the bank if they avoid hidden costs like excessive support or over-engineering. Whether you choose Community’s free flexibility or Enterprise’s robust features, planning is key.

Upgrading odoo can be expensive but careful planning can make it more affordable. While costs depend on customizations, hosting choices and partner support, businesses can minimize costs with smart decisions. If you’re considering an odoo upgrade, assess your needs and to optimize your investment. Get in touch

items.title: Exploring JavaScript Inheritance: Understanding Prototypes and Classes
items.author: Beatris Ilieva
items.content:
Inheritance allows us to pass properties and methods from one object to another. Let's get as an example the objects and . Each person have properties like and and methods like and . Does the have them? Sure, an employee IS a person. Thus, instead of duplicating the person's characteristics and behaviors into the employee object, we can follow the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle. By doing that, we decrease the quantity of the code and respectively the amount of potentials bugs while keeping it easy to extend, read and understand.

Inheritance is key feature of OOP (Object-oriented programming). OOP is at the heart of languages such as Java, C# and Python. Everything revolves around classes and objects, and we must use OOP principles to write most applications. In contrast, JavaScript (JS) is a prototype-based language. A prototype inheritance is way to link objects in a so they can use each other's (properties and methods) . Every object is JS has its prototype.

Imagine you are at work and you have forgotten your charger. You ask a colleague to borrow theirs. In this case, you do not have your own charger, but you use one as if it was your own. Does that mean that the charger get's copied? No, there is only one charger - the one of your colleague (your prototype). After your device is charged, you return the charger.

From this, we can confidently embrace the nearly philosophical notion that, in OOP inheritance, the relationship is defined by what an object , whereas in prototypal inheritance, the relationship is defined by what an object .

The prototype chain and OOP inheritance are one-way relationships -> prototypes and parent classes do not have access to the members of their descendants.

A prototype is a reference to another object, and it doesn't consume additional memory. Objects are linked by reference, making it memory efficient.

Let's build a prototype chain using the devices , , , and .

items.title: Breaking Monoliths: Transforming Legacy Codebase with NX Monorepo
items.author: Yevheni Kshevitskyi
items.content:
In any long-running software project, architecture is key to scalability, maintainability, and a smooth developer experience. This article dives into the story of a bold architectural shift that transformed a legacy monolith into a sleek, modular system—boosting flexibility, future-proofing the codebase, and making development a whole lot more enjoyable 🚀.

The Challenge: A Legacy Codebase Under Pressure

The legacy project in question had been in operation for approximately four years. Over time, it evolved into a multi-app frontend ecosystem built atop a modern stack—primarily Vue—integrated with REST APIs. While the setup allowed for rapid prototyping and delivery, it also became a bottleneck as the project scaled. The following issues surfaced:

  • : Adding new apps slowed builds and affected performance.
  • Development Experience (DX): With multiple developers working on the same codebase, merge conflicts became a regular occurrence.
  • : Unexpected bugs surfaced due to intertwined dependencies and a lack of clear boundaries.
  • : A monolithic structure hindered the team's ability to iterate and grow efficiently.

These challenges made it clear that the project's architecture needed a serious rethink.

Researching Solutions: Modularization with NX monorepo

Determined to address these pain points, I began researching ways to modularize the codebase while adhering to domain-driven design (DDD) principles. The goal was to split the monolithic codebase into self-contained, maintainable modules without sacrificing the integrity of the system.

My research led me to , a powerful tool designed for scalable application development. NX offered:

  • : Clear separation of apps and libraries into distinct domains.
  • : Incremental builds and caching reduced build times.
  • : Logical module boundaries reduced merge conflicts and allowed teams to work independently.
  • : Built-in support for advanced frontend frameworks like Vue and Svelte, combined with testing and linting capabilities.

The Proof of Concept: Bringing the Team on Board

To validate the potential benefits of NX, I created a proof-of-concept (PoC) demonstrating how the legacy codebase could be restructured. The PoC showcased:

  • Clear separation of features into libraries aligned with DDD principles.
  • Incremental builds, which significantly reduced build times.
  • Simplified collaboration, as each team could now focus on their module without affecting others.

Presenting the PoC to the team was a —it got everyone aligned on the vision for a more modular, scalable future.

Making It Happen: The Migration 🚀

Following the approval of the PoC, the team embarked on implementing the NX monorepo structure. The transformation involved:

  • : Gradually moving apps and shared functionality into NX libraries.
  • : Aligning components and services with DDD principles.
  • : Leveraging NX's caching and dependency graph to improve build and test processes.

This change had a huge impact:

  • : Developers reported fewer merge conflicts and an overall smoother workflow.
  • : Incremental builds slashed build times significantly ⚡.
  • : The modular architecture made it easier to onboard new apps and developers.

This journey underscores the importance of re-evaluating architectural decisions as projects evolve. By introducing NX into the legacy project, we:

  • Shifted from a monolithic, hard-to-scale structure to a modular, domain-driven architecture.
  • Future-proofed our frontend, making it more maintainable and adaptable.
  • Boosted developer productivity and collaboration in ways that were immediately felt.

For teams struggling with monolithic setups, embracing a monorepo strategy like NX can be a total game-changer. The migration might take effort, but the long-term benefits—for both the codebase and the people working on it—are 💡

items.title: 🚀 Spring Boot Pro Tip: The Hidden Power of @TransactionalEventListener!
items.author: Rachid
items.content:
Did you know that Spring Boot allows you to execute code after a transaction is committed using @TransactionalEventListener? Most developers use @EventListener, but this hidden gem ensures your logic only runs if the transaction succeeds.💡 Problem: When Events Fire Too Early Imagine you're saving a user and sending a welcome email before the transaction commits:

@Component
public class UserEventListener {
    @EventListener
    public void handleUserCreated(UserCreatedEvent event) {
        emailService.sendWelcomeEmail(event.getUser()); // 🚨 Runs even if transaction fails!
    }
}

👉 If the database transaction fails, the email is still sent, leading to inconsistencies. 😱

✅ Solution: @TransactionalEventListener

@Component
public class UserEventListener {
    @TransactionalEventListener
    public void handleUserCreated(UserCreatedEvent event) {
        emailService.sendWelcomeEmail(event.getUser()); // ✅ Runs ONLY if the transaction is committed!
    }
}

🚀 Now, the event fires only if the transaction succeeds!🛠️ How to Use It in a Spring Boot App 1️⃣ Publish an Event Inside a Transaction

@Service
public class UserService {
    @Autowired private ApplicationEventPublisher publisher;
    @Autowired private UserRepository userRepository;

    @Transactional
    public void createUser(String name, String email) {
        User user = userRepository.save(new User(name, email));
        publisher.publishEvent(new UserCreatedEvent(user)); // 🚀 Event is published
    }
}

2️⃣ Listen to the Event After Transaction Commits

@Component
public class UserEventListener {
    @TransactionalEventListener
    public void handleUserCreated(UserCreatedEvent event) {
        System.out.println("User created: " + event.getUser().getEmail());
    }
}

✅ Guaranteed execution only if the transaction is successful. Prevents sending emails, messages, or notifications for failed transactions.

Ensures event-driven logic only executes when the database is consistent.

Perfect for logging, auditing, and triggering external services.
